title.rendered: Project Coordinator, Sr. School Mental Health Officer, Advocacy Officer, Psychosocial Officer

content.rendered:
The Centre for Mental Health and Counselling–Nepal (CMC-Nepal) is a national-level non-governmental organization (NGO) dedicated to promoting mental health and psychosocial well-being. CMC-Nepal works to ensure access to quality Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS) services, social inclusion, and the protection of the rights of individuals with mental health conditions and psychosocial disabilities. We are pleased to announce an exciting opportunity to join our team in various positions in the Karnali Mental Health Program-Phase III (KMHP III).

The KMHP III will be implemented in 12 rural/municipalities of Dailekh, Rukum West, and Jumla district in partnership with CBM Global. The overall objective of the program is “Persons with or at risk of mental health conditions and psychosocial disabilities enjoy independent, dignified lives, and are fully included in their communities on an equal basis with others.” The program focuses on strengthening health systems, promoting school mental health, expanding community-based psychosocial support, advancing advocacy initiatives, and promoting disability inclusion.

CMC-Nepal invites applications from qualified, competent, and committed Nepali citizens for the following positions:

Position: Project Coordinator – 1 Position

Duty Station: Karnali Province Office, Surkhet (with frequent field visits)

Position Summary: The Project Coordinator will lead the overall planning, implementation, coordination, and reporting of KMHP. The position is responsible for ensuring effective collaboration with local governments, key stakeholders, and CBM Global to achieve quality and timely project outcomes.

The role also includes staff mobilization, stakeholder coordination, and systematic documentation and reporting of project interventions. The Project Coordinator will work closely with local government authorities, Organizations of Persons with Disabilities (OPDs), NFDN, CBM Global, and other relevant stakeholders to ensure effective and efficient implementation of the project. The person will be responsible to the Program Manager

Key Responsibilities:

  • Lead project planning, implementation, monitoring, and reporting in line with project objectives and timelines
  • Ensure effective coordination and collaboration with local governments, partner organizations, OPDs, CBM Global and other stakeholders
  • Oversee timely implementation of activities and efficient utilization of resources
  • Provide technical and managerial support to the project team, including problem-solving and guidance
  • Mobilize and manage project staff to ensure a cohesive, motivated, and high-performing team
  • Facilitate stakeholder engagement, advocacy initiatives, and representation in relevant forums and clusters
  • Contribute to advocacy efforts and integration of mental health into local systems
  • Build team capacity and provide support during humanitarian or emergency contexts

Qualifications & Experience:

  • Master’s degree in psychology, Counselling Psychology, Public Health, Social Work, Sociology, Development Studies, or related field
  • Minimum 3 years of progressive experience in project management, preferably in MHPSS and disability inclusion projects
  • Strong coordination, leadership, and stakeholder engagement skills
  • Experience working with government systems, OPDs, and multi-sector stakeholders
  • Excellent reporting and communication skills (English and Nepali)
  • Proficiency in MS Office applications

Position: Sr. School Mental Health Officer -1 Position

Duty Station: Karnali Province Office, Surkhet (with frequent field visits)

Position Summary: The Sr. School Mental Health Officer will lead the implementation, monitoring, and quality assurance of school mental health interventions under the project. The position is responsible for strengthening school-based MHPSS services through capacity building of teachers, regular supervision and mentoring of school personnel, and the establishment and strengthening of school counseling systems.

The person will ensure coordination with local governments, education stakeholders, and communities to promote a safe, inclusive, and supportive learning environment for children and adolescents. Sr. School Mental Health Officer will also oversee documentation, reporting, and technical support to ensure the sustainability and integration of school mental health services within the local education system.

The person will be responsible to the Project Coordinator.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Design and implement school-based mental health and psychosocial programs
  • Conduct training, supervision, and coaching for teachers on psychosocial support and counseling
  • Support establishment and strengthening of school counseling units
  • Facilitate activities such as adolescent health awareness, life skills education, and parental psychoeducation
  • Coordinate with schools, local governments, and education stakeholders
  • Ensure documentation, reporting, and quality assurance of school mental health activities

Qualifications & Experience:

  • PGD in School Counseling, master’s degree in psychology, Social Sciences, or related field
  • Minimum 3 years of relevant experience in school mental health or MHPSS programs
  • Experience in training facilitation, supervision, and mentoring
  • Strong communication, coordination, and reporting skills
  • Proven ability to work with children, teachers, and communities

Position: Advocacy Officer-1 Position

Duty Station: Karnali Province Office, Surkhet (with frequent field visits)

Position Summary:The Advocacy Officer will lead the implementation, and coordination of advocacy and policy engagement initiatives to promote mental health and psychosocial disability inclusion at provincial and local levels. The position is responsible for conducting policy analysis, identifying gaps in existing laws and systems, and developing evidence-based advocacy strategies and campaigns.

The person will develop a partnership framework with local Organizations of Persons with Disabilities and support their organizational capacity building. The position will represent the organization in relevant forums, strengthen partnerships, document advocacy outcomes and learning, and ensure that mental health and psychosocial disability issues are effectively integrated into local governance systems and development agendas.

The role involves close coordination with government stakeholders, Organizations of Persons with Disabilities (OPDs), Self-Help Groups (SHGs), civil society organizations, and media to influence policy and programmatic changes. The Advocacy Officer will support awareness and de-stigmatization efforts, facilitate meaningful participation of rights holders, and contribute to the development of Information, Education, and Communication (IEC) materials. The person is responsible to the Project Coordinator.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Lead advocacy and policy engagement initiatives on mental health and psychosocial disability at provincial and local levels, including policy review and strategy development
  • Design and implement advocacy campaigns, awareness programs, and de-stigmatization activities using media and community mobilization
  • Facilitate empowerment and meaningful participation of persons with mental health conditions, OPDs, and self-help groups in advocacy processes
  • Maintain strong coordination and partnerships with government authorities, health offices, NGOs, CBOs, and other key stakeholders
  • Represent the organization in relevant provincial and local forums, networks, and coordination platforms
  • Supervise, mentor, and support self-advocates and field teams, ensuring effective field activities, reporting, and documentation

Qualifications & Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree (master’s preferred) in Social Science, Political Science, Community Development, or a related field
  • Minimum 3 years of professional experience in advocacy, policy engagement, communications, or project implementation, preferably in the NGO/INGO or development sector, with a focus on social issues, mental health, or disability rights
  • Demonstrated experience working and coordinating with government bodies, local authorities, and community stakeholders, including OPDs and SHGs
  • Strong skills in coordination, facilitation, communication, and stakeholder engagement
  • Excellent documentation, reporting, and analytical writing skills
  • Proven ability to work in multidisciplinary and multi-stakeholder environments to support program implementation and advocacy initiatives

Position: Psychosocial Officer-1 Position

Duty Station: Jumla District

Position Summary: The primary objective of the position is to provide mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS) and counseling. It aims to improve the mental health and psychosocial well-being of these individuals, including persons with disabilities and those with, or at risk of, mental health conditions and psychosocial disabilities in the project areas. The individual will be responsible for conducting one-to-one and group counselling, family sensitization, community awareness activities, coordination, and reporting on MHPSS responses for CMC-Nepal, as designated. The person is responsible to the Mental Health Coordinator.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Provide individual and group psychosocial counseling, including follow-up support and referrals at the community level
  • Conduct community awareness sessions, orientations, and family sensitization on mental health, psychosocial well-being, disability inclusion, disaster response, and self-care
  • Support formation and mobilization of peer groups and self-help groups to promote co-recovery and community-based psychosocial support
  • Link persons with mental health conditions, psychosocial disabilities, and vulnerable populations with relevant social protection schemes and disability benefits
  • Support CPSWs and community workers in planning, implementation, and reporting of community-based MHPSS activities in coordination with program teams
  • Ensure effective coordination with stakeholders for referral services and continuity of care
  • Maintain proper documentation, reporting, and data management of all MHPSS interventions and field activities

Qualifications & Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in psychology or social work or health education or public health, with a six-month psychosocial counselling training (NHTC or recognized organization).
  • Minimum three years of experience as a psychosocial counsellor, preferably in community-based MHPSS projects.
  • Strong understanding of psychosocial interventions, leadership, coordination, and communication skills.
  • Basic computer skills (MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint), email proficiency, and report writing in English and Nepali.
  • Motivated to work in multicultural and challenging environments, able to work multi-sectoral, build effective relationships, and undertake field visits.
  • CMC-Nepal is committed to safeguarding children and vulnerable adults and to the implementation of the Children and Vulnerable Adult Safeguarding Policy. It strongly adheres to the Prevention of Sexual
  • Exploitation, Harassment, and Abuse (PSEAH) Policy. Commitment to safeguarding and PSEAH will be assessed during the interview and reference checks of the applicant.

Salaries and benefits: As per the organizational norms.

Candidates, particularly those from disadvantaged, socially excluded, or marginalized groups including females, persons with disabilities, and ethnic minorities are strongly encouraged to apply.

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further selection process. Applications will be reviewed, and candidates will be selected on a rolling basis until the position is filled. The selected candidate must not have any criminal records. All rights related to recruitment, selection, and cancellation (if required) are reserved by CMC-Nepal.

Interested candidates meeting the above requirements are requested to submit their application, along with an updated CV, to the following address no later than 23 April 2026.

Executive Director

CMC-Nepal

PO Box Number 5295

Kathmandu, Nepal

Email: recruitment@cmcnepal.org.np

Website: www.cmcnepal.org.np

title.rendered: SAMVAD Associate, Social Mobilizer, CBNO and Advocacy Coordinator, MEAL Officer, Admin and Finance Officer, Climate Justice Technician

content.rendered:
Group of Helping Hands (SAHAS) Nepal is a non-profit making non-governmental-organization founded in 1996 since then engaging social mobilization, grassroots organisation/Community-Based Network Organisation (CBNO) formation and enhancing their capacity through partnership and implementing diverse programme and projects in the sectors of sustainable agriculture, livelihood, food security, climate justice, advocacy and lobby, quality education, youth development in 21 districts of Nepal.

SAHAS Nepal is implementing the “Advancing Climate Resilient Communities (HOPE) Project” in Jajarkot district. This project aims to improve the lives of climate of vulnerable communities in Karnali province by promoting social and economic inclusion. It strengthens community climate resilience through climate resilient agriculture, diversified livelihoods and community-based disaster risk reduction while empowering communities to drive climate justice and sustainable development.

SAHAS Nepal is looking the potential candidates for the following positions:

Position: CBNO and Advocacy Coordinator

Duty station: West Rukum district

Required number: 1

Academic qualification/experiences:

  • Master’s degree in Humanities, Social Sciences, Social work, Rural Development from recognized university with five years experiences or Bachelors degree with eight years of experience in social mobilization and community development
  • Experiences of working in lobby, advocacy, social, economic and cultural rights
  • Experiences of collection of issues, documentation, case study in local contexts
  • Good coordination skills and efficient team player
  • Excellent writing report and communication in English and Nepali

Key responsibilities:

  • Supervise and support for the formation of Community Based Organisation (CBO), Main Committees (MCs) and Community Based Network Organisations (CBNOs)Strengthen CBNOs capacity to lobby and advocacy, and social, economic and cultural rights
  • Linkage among CBNOs, CBOs, MCs and Rural/Municipality and non-government organization for resources leverage
  • Provide technical support to the district project team in planning, implementation, monitoring and reporting of the activities as per project’s objectives
  • Review project budget, track the expenditure of budget regularly
  • Provide technical backstopping to strengthen Local partner (LNGO)

Position: Monitoring Evaluation Accountability and Learning (MEAL) Officer

Duty Station: Jajarkot district

Required number: 1

Academic qualification/experiences:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Social Science/Rural Development or equivalent from a recognized university with at least 2 years of working experiences in monitoring, evaluation, accountability and learning (MEAL)
  • Good coordination skills and efficient team player
  • Good knowledge on program monitoring and data collections, data analysis and reporting
  • Proficiency in MS Office and demonstrated skills in statistical packages such as SPSS
  • Excellent writing report and communication in English and Nepali

Key responsibilities:

  • Assist project team in project planning, adopting monitoring tools, setting up timelines and measuring progress
  • Support the project team in documenting results and learnings, and ensure all reporting requirements are fulfilled in a timely manner
  • Organize capacity building of project staff on M&E methodologies and other relevant trainings as per the requirement
  • Work closely with Project Officers and Coordinators to ensure that impact, outcome and output level indicators are reported and monitored
  • Collection of relevant data, development of case studies, pictures and video documentation
  • Coordination baseline, midline and end-line survey and prepare reports

Position: Admin and Finance Officer

Duty Station: Jajarkot district

Required number: 1

Academic qualification/experiences:

  • Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Commerce, Management or related field
  • A minimum of three years of relevant experience in a similar position in I/NGO
  • Sound knowledge of accounting software like Moneyworks/FAMAS or similar would be an added advantage.
  • Proficiency in MS Office especially in Excel
  • Ability to work in multi-cultural and multi-dimensional team
  • Sound financial management skills, attention to details and mathematical proficiency
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English and Nepali

Key responsibilities:

  • Prepare and analyse accounting records, financial statements, and other financial reports to assess accuracy, completeness, and comply with reporting and procedural standards.
  • Prepare vouchers and record transaction in the accounting system after obtaining proper approvals.
  • Prepare periodic account reconciliations, income and expenditures reports and budget vs. actual expenses report for the project
  • Prepare monthly and quarterly financial report under appropriate activities
  • Prepare advance request, travel authorization and settlement
  • Comply with local tax laws and regulations
  • Facilitate in procurement, field staff movements and employee leave records
  • Maintain keeping inventories of fixed and consumables and physical count
  • Assist Project Coordinator, District team and finance team-central in budgeting and cash flow projection of field office
  • Monitor project site for proper maintenance of documentation
  • Financial monitoring and support to strengthen the financial management of Local Partner

Position: Climate Justice Technician

Duty Station: Jajarkot district

Required number: 2

Academic qualification/experiences:

  • Intermediate or 10+2 in Agriculture (JTA) from recognized university or equivalent with at least two years working experience in relevant sectors
  • Willing to work in remote rural municipalities with disadvantaged groups
  • Knowledge of activities planning, reporting and monitoring of agriculture, social mobilization and community development program
  • Experience of counseling, advocacy of various social issues including climate justice a with local dialect

Key responsibilities:

  • Support for the formation of CBOs, MCs and CBNOs
  • Planning, implementation, monitoring and reporting of the project activities
  • Good coordination and management with local government, NGOs and communities
  • Organize project related trainings and advocacy
  • Prepare report with documentation of various advocacy issues

Position: Social Mobilizer

Duty Station: Jajarkot district

Required number: 4

Academic qualification/experiences:

  • 10+2 passed or equivalent with two years working experiences in advocacy and lobby, facilitators, human rights and social mobilize fields
  • Experience of counseling of social justice and gender equity with local dialect
  • Committed and interested to work in remote communities with facilitation, communications skills, engagement and local languages
  • Knowledge of activities planning, reporting, monitoring, social mobilization coordination
  • Ability to conduct training, data collection and report writing skills

Key responsibilities:

  • Promoting Gender Equity and Social Justice
  • Support for the formation of CBOs, MCs and CBNOs groups
  • Organize CBOs meeting and capacity building training
  • Conduct various capacity building trainings and workshops
  • Manage project activities’ survey and data collections
  • Implementation and documentation of project planned activities

Position: SAMVAD Associate

Duty Station: Jajarkot district

Required number: 1

Academic qualification/experiences:

  • 10+2 passed with one year working experience in SAMVAD
  • Skills of Facilitation technical backstopping
  • Knowledge of advocacy campaigns and social awareness
  • Ability to organize SAMVAD led activities
  • Communication skills, sincere and flexible on working hour

Key responsibilities:

  • Support for SAMVAD group formation
  • Plan and implement SAMVAD activities
  • Manage and support SAMVAD animators for facilitating SAMVAD Centers
  • Organize capacity building and trainings for SAMVAD adolescent girls
  • Conduct advocacy campaigns and awareness sessions for SAMVAD
  • Communication and coordination with local government and like-minded organizations

Salary and Benefits: As per the rules and regulations of SAHAS Nepal.

Interested Nepali citizens are requested to send their updated CV with cover letter showing their interest including mailing address and telephone number along with two references to Founder and Executive Director by 24th April 2026 to sahasnepalvacancy@gmail.com.

Telephone enquiries will not be entertained. Only short-listed candidates will be contacted for further screening process. SAHAS Nepal reserves the right to reject any or all applications without assigning any reasons.

SAHAS Nepal promotes workforce diversity and applies positive decimation to candidates from discriminated groups. Applications from and members of indigenous and disadvantaged groups are highly encouraged.

(“SAHAS Nepal is committed to safeguarding and a zero-tolerance organization related to abuse, corruption, bribery, fraud.”)

Human Development and Community Services (HDCS) is a NGO working in three main sectors of health, education, and community development since 1991. Currently, it manages various projects across Nepal especially in rural areas and provides services through hospitals, health camps, public health programs, disaster preparedness and response, school development programs, teacher training, educational daycare centers for children with special needs and community radio stations.

Please apply before April 22, 2026 and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the interview.

HDCS is looking for committed and service-minded Nepali citizens for the following:

Position: Ophthalmic Assistant
No. of Vacancy: 1

Qualification and experience: Respective degree from recognized university or institution with registration of respective council or regulatory body and minimum 1 year working experience.

Aasaman Nepal, in partnership with Welthungerhilfe, is implementing the Civil society for nature-based local agri-food-systems (CS4FS) which aims to improve the food security and resilience of 3000 marginalized households (15000 persons) in three municipalities of Dhanusa District.

The project contributes to the promotion of local agri-food systems through nature-based solutions and policy localization led by civil society organizations in Madhesh Province, Nepal. It is intended that 3,000 marginalized households have transitioned towards a regenerative agriculture production at the project end, civil society actors from the agricultural sector promote and advocate regenerative agriculture at municipal and provincial level and 3 local governments support policy and programmes in agriculture in line with the agro-ecological principles (municipal/provincial level).

Aasaman Nepal is looking for self-motivated, result-oriented, and hard-working Nepali citizens for the following positions:

Position: Advocacy and Livelihood Officer (Dhanusha Based)-Nos. 1

The position is based on the working Municipality of Dhanusha district with frequent field (approximately 70%) Visit. The position directly reports to Project Coordinator.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Knowledge/skills on coordinating and working with Palikas/wards.
  • Understanding of the local political environment and key policies issues related to Livelihood, nutrition and agriculture.
  • Skills in designing and conducting discussion sessions and evidence-based policy advocacy mainly on nutrition and agriculture. Facilitate groups/targeted communities to link them with Palikas and wards.
  • Empower and support marginalized and vulnerable groups, including women, youth, and people with disabilities, in advocating for their rights and needs in relation to Livelihood, nutrition and agriculture policies.
  • Lead or support the design and implementation of public awareness campaigns aimed at educating local populations about the importance of nutrition, sustainable agriculture practices, and policy changes.
  • Build and strengthen partnerships with local government, private sector, and service providers to support regenerative agriculture.
  • Generate periodic reports, learning document.
  • Develop and disseminate advocacy materials, such as policy briefs, position papers, and success stories, to promote key messages and influence the opinion and decision-making processes.
  • Identify and assess the livelihood and enterprise needs of landless and marginalized families; facilitate their access to on-farm, off-farm, and non-farm income opportunities including goat farming, poultry, bamboo-based businesses, and handicraft enterprises, in consultation with target groups.
  • Support landless and marginalized households to access public and private land for leasehold farming, kitchen gardening, and community-based agricultural activities.
  • Mobilize and strengthen Community Based Organizations (CBOs) to engage in evidence-based policy advocacy at municipal and provincial levels on livelihood, land rights, nutrition, and agriculture.
  • Facilitate linkages between landless families and relevant government schemes, social protection programs, vocational training providers, input suppliers, and markets to strengthen their livelihood and enterprise base, with particular focus on goat farming, poultry, bamboo products, and handicraft value chains.
  • Support the formation and capacity building of savings and credit groups, cooperatives, and CBOs to promote collective livelihood actions and community-led resource mobilization.
  • Document and share livelihood success stories, best practices, and field learnings to contribute to knowledge management and policy advocacy efforts of the project.
  • Support the development, implementation, and monitoring of enterprise plans for target households engaged in goat farming, poultry rearing, bamboo-based enterprises, and handicraft businesses; coordinate with the Enterprise Development Officer for technical backstopping and resource linkages.
  • Advocate for CBO- and community-level policies that support small enterprise development, including access to revolving funds, cooperative membership, and local government grants for landless households engaged in livestock, bamboo, and handicraft value chains.
  • Ensure cross-cutting issues such as gender and inclusion mainstreamed throughout the project cycle

Qualifications and experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in social science, rural development, agriculture, or relevant field, with at least 3 years of working experience in advocacy, livelihood promotion, community-based enterprise development, or relevant field. Experience in supporting livestock (goat/poultry), bamboo, or handicraft enterprises is an added advantage.
  • Self-motivated and have a good learning attitude.
  • Should have skill set on rights-based approach, gender and inclusion and PLA (Participatory Learning and Action)
  • Familiarity to the accountability tools i.e., community sore card (CSC), public hearing
  • Good communication skills both in English and Nepali languages.Open and believes in teamwork.
  • Fluency in Maithali languages will be a plus point.
  • A valid motorbike license holder is preferred.

Languages: Fluent in Nepali, English, and the local language (Maithili) is preferred or required.

Aasaman Nepal has zero tolerance for the abuse and exploitation of vulnerable people. We expect all our employees and volunteers to ensure we are protecting children, young people, and vulnerable adults from harm and abide by our safeguarding policy.

Other attributes for all positions are excellent quality of work and commitment to high standards; strong communication skills; team building; willingness and motivation for good service delivery; strong networking and interpersonal skills; and being comfortable working under stress and emergencies.

Age Limit: Candidates between 21 and 55 years old are only eligible to apply for the above position, depending upon the nature of the job they will carry.

All employees must adhere to the child’s safeguarding provisions covered in the project agreement.

Monitor staff and partners’ and ensure adherence to Aasaman Nepal’s policies and internal procedures, protocols, and code of conduct, with special attention to safeguarding standards and procedures, the case handling framework, child protection, and do no harm policies. Also, immediate reporting and redress of any complaints and issues based on their severity.

Notes:

  • Locals, especially women, and members of ethnic or marginalized communities are encouraged to apply for this position.
  • Applications received after the deadline will not be considered, and only shortlisted candidates will be notified for further processing.
  • Canvassing at any stage of the recruitment process shall lead to the automatic disqualification of the candidate.

Interested candidates are requested to submit their filled-out application by downloading the link below along with an updated CV and cover letter by April 23, 2026.

https://forms.gle/yiC47JFY9d4qKc7eA

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for participation in the recruitment process. No telephone inquiry is entertained. ASN reserves the right to qualify or disqualify any application without any reason.

Background: Manas is a UK-based international organisation committed to promoting healing-centred responses to mass violence. We are committed to support institutions by embedding further psychological insights within their practice, which results in more effective ways to disrupt cycles of violence allowing for recovery to take place. We have an exciting opportunity within our team so would like to invite applications for the following:

Position: Administration and Finance support officer

Location: Nepal, remote work with flexible working arrangements Position type: 1 consultancy position for (0.2 equivalent, flexible)

The 3-year project focuses on improving the psychosocial wellbeing of conflict victims through equitable access to quality MHPSS during the Transitional Justice in Nepal, funded by the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC) and in partnership with Centre for mental Health and Counselling Nepal (CMC-Nepal).

Responsibilities will include accounting, budgeting, book-keeping, reporting, financial transactions, and some logistical management following Manas’ administrative and financial policies and procedures that are UK-based and, at times, project-based depending on the implementation of the activities.

Requirements:

  • Master Degree in Business Administration, Accountancy, or Management with at least 2 years of work experience, and/or 5 years of work experience post
    Bachelor degree in finance and administration in a Civil Society Organisation (CSO) or a Non-Governmental Organisation.
  • Excellent command of the English language is essential, both spoken and written.
  • Extensive experience in accounting standards, maintaining books of account, financial reporting, internal control management, payment of invoices.
  • Well versed in the use of Microsoft Word and Excel.
  • Proven competence in general logistical and administrative work.
  • Ability to work independently, flexibly, and with confidence in intercultural interpersonal relationships with a team of international experts from varied cultural backgrounds

Desirable:

  • Good knowledge of accounting software, such as Money Works Gold

Personal Attributes:

  • Commitment to MANAS’ vision, mission, and values
  • Honesty, openness, and integrity in professional relationships
  • Flexibility and adaptability to changing priorities and situations
  • Willingness to learn and develop new skills.

Equal Opportunities

MANAS is committed to Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion [See our ‘ED I sta te me n t’ ]. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates regardless of age, disability, gender identity or sexual orientation, marital status, race, religion/belief, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, religion, political opinion, trade union membership or disability.

We aim to ensure fair treatment and no applicant or employee receives less favorable treatment on any protected grounds under the ‘Equality Act 2010’ (UK Law).

Application

Interested candidates who feel they meet the above criteria, can email their application with their latest CV and a short cover letter highlighting their skills and experience to work for the organisation. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.

Only short-listed candidates will be invited for an interview. Manas reserves the rights for recruitment, selection, and cancellation (if relevant) of candidates for the recruitment of this position.

Contacts

Applications are to be sent to the following email: Recruitment@manas.org.uk

Website: www.manas.org.uk

content.rendered:
The Centre for Mental Health and Counselling – Nepal (CMC-Nepal) is a national level non-governmental organization (NGO) dedicated to promoting mental health and psychosocial wellbeing. We are pleased to announce an exciting opportunity to join our team as a Field Coordinator cum Psychologist in the ‘Empowering Inclusion and Enhancing Accessibility for Person with Disabilities’ project.

The project is jointly implemented by CMC-Nepal and HRENDC in Chhayanath Rara Municipality and Khatyad Rural Municipality in Mugu district. It is funded and supported by Felm-Nepal. The project seeks to enhance the well-being of communities in the target locations by promoting social and economic inclusion, empowering them to lead transformative change toward justice, equitable, and sustainable societies. This project further contributes in building aaccess to inclusive quality education.

It therefore seeks candidates for the following positions:

Field Coordinator cum Psychologist (FCP)- 1 full-time position, based in Gamgadhi, Mugu with field visits to project locations

Position Overview: The FCP is responsible for designing, planning, implementing, monitoring, and reporting project activities, as well as coordinating and collaborating with local partners, local government, Organization of Persons with Disabilities (OPDs), Felm-Nepal, and other concerned stakeholders for the effective implementation of the project. The Field Coordinator cum Psychologist is expected to provide technical and project management support to the project team members in the implementation of project activities at field level.

Duties and responsibilities of the position:

  • Collaborate with the project team, including partner staff to develop plans aligned with the project document, ensuring activities are implemented on time and within set objectives
  • Lead the overall implementation of the project ensuring effectiveness and quality delivery of the project interventions
  • Provide training and supervision to the teachers, health service providers, community leaders, CBOs on student psychosocial counselling, basic psychosocial support, disability inclusion and child protection
  • Establish and operationalize school-based counselling units
  • Support in identification of children with functional limitations and provide direct psychosocial support and counselling to children
  • Mobilize and manage project staff efficiently, ensuring a cohesive and motivated project team
  • Support to the team members to address the challenges related to the project implementation that included but not limited to planning, implementation, data/information recording and reporting in close coordination with National Expert and ED.
  • Ensure that procedures and supporting documents are properly reviewed and approved, budget utilization is regularly tracked and budget is effectively utilized
  • Ensure the organizational safeguarding, PSEAH, and anti-corruption principles are mainstreamed in all intervention and functions of the project
  • Collaborate with the MEAL Counterpart to ensure inclusive and gender sensitive MEAL mechanism in the project.
  • Extend coordination and collaboration with local partner, local government, provincial government, Organization of Persons with Disabilities (OPDs), Felm-Nepal and other concerned stakeholders for the effective implementation of the project

Requirements:

  • Master’s degree in Psychology or Counselling Psychology or Student Counselling from recognized university with least 3 years’ experience in school based psychosocial intervention and community based MHPSS work
  • Having strong competence in project management, leadership, evaluation/ implementation research networking and coordination
  • Practical and strong background in supervising project team members in mental health and psychosocial perspective will be advantage for the post.
  • Having good comprehension and learning attitude in mental health, preferably in child and adolescent mental health, psychosocial interventions and disability inclusion
  • Experience in coordination with multi-stakeholders (GOs/I/NGOs/OPDs) for the effectiveness of implementation of the project activities and its upscaling and sustainability
  • Good report writing, documentation and communication skills in both English and Nepali language
  • Demonstrated ability to work in a multi-cultural environment and establish harmonious and effective working relationships within and outside the organization
    Willing to be based in Mugu district, remote location of Karnali Province and travel in the project areas
  • CMC-Nepal is committed to safeguarding children and vulnerable adults and to the implementation of the Children and Vulnerable Adult Safeguarding Policy. It strongly adheres to the Prevention of Sexual Exploitation, Harassment, and Abuse (PSEAH) Policy. Commitment to safeguarding and PSEAH will be assessed during the interview and reference checks of the applicant.

Salaries and benefits: As per the organizational norms.

Candidates, particularly those from disadvantaged, socially excluded, or marginalized groups including females, persons with disabilities, and ethnic minorities are strongly encouraged to apply.

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further selection process. Applications will be reviewed, and candidates will be selected on a rolling basis until the position is filled. The selected candidate must not have any criminal records. All rights related to recruitment, selection, and cancellation (if required) are reserved by CMC-Nepal.

Interested candidates meeting the above requirements are requested to submit their application, along with an updated CV, to the following address no later than 23 April 2026.

Executive Director

CMC-Nepal

PO Box Number 5295

Kathmandu, Nepal

Email: recruitment@cmcnepal.org.np, Website: www.cmcnepal.org.np

Aasaman Nepal is implementing SRHR and Gender equality/women empowerment including ending child marriage and harmful practices Programs in Madhesh Province, in the partnership with UNFPA. The overarching objectives of the programs are to enhance reproductive health outcomes, eliminate gender-based violence, empower women economically and socially, and reduce child marriage. The integrated approach aims to provide comprehensive reproductive health services, promote gender equity, facilitate women’s economic empowerment through skill development and entrepreneurship, and contribute to the global effort of ending child marriage. These programs collectively strive to create a society where individuals, particularly women and adolescents, have the knowledge, resources, and opportunities to make informed decisions about their health, well-being, and future.

Aasaman Nepal is looking for the self-motivated, result oriented and hard-working Nepali citizen for the following position:

Position Title: MEAL Coordinator

Project/Program: Empowered Women Prosperous Nepal (EWPN), Ending Child Marriage (ECM)

Organization: Aasaman Nepal

Location: Janakpur, Dhanusha

Reporting to: Program Manager

Partner: United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA)

Position Overview:

The MEAL Coordinator will be responsible for overseeing the monitoring, evaluation, accountability, and learning components of the EWPN, and ECM programs implemented by Aasaman Nepal in partnership with UNFPA. The MEAL Coordinator will play a key role in ensuring the quality and effectiveness of the programs through robust data management, compliance with UNFPA requirements, regular reporting, and knowledge sharing. The role includes designing and implementing monitoring frameworks, conducting evaluations, facilitating learning, and ensuring accountability across project activities.

Key Responsibilities:

Monitoring and Evaluation

  • Develop and implement a comprehensive MEAL framework and tools to monitor program progress and outcomes in alignment with UNFPA standards.
  • Assess the effectiveness of Rupantaran training, GTA training, roll out family dialogue sessions by analyzing changes in participants’ knowledge and behavior.
  • Evaluate the impact of Comprehensive Sexuality Education (CSE) training provided to teachers and health workers.
  • Conduct regular field visits to monitor project activities, ensuring adherence to quality standards and gathering feedback from beneficiaries.
  • Establish data collection systems to track key indicators for Gender Equality, Disability, and Social Inclusion (GEDSI), Gender Transformative Approach (GTA), and Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR) training sessions and events.
  • Support the establishment of Adolescent-Friendly Information Corners (AFICs) and Adolescent-Friendly Health Services (AFHS) by monitoring their functionality and utilization.
  • Conduct periodic assessments and evaluations to measure the effectiveness and impact of project interventions.
  • Oversee the operation and management of safe houses by tracking service utilization, beneficiary satisfaction and compliance with safety and protection protocols.
  • Monitor linkages between safe houses and One-Stop Crisis Management Centers (OCMCs) to ensure coordinated support services for survivors.

Accountability

  • Ensure that accountability mechanisms are in place for beneficiaries and stakeholders, including feedback and complaint-handling systems.
  • Facilitate quarterly beneficiary meetings to ensure program relevance, responsiveness, and continuous improvement.
  • Engage with community members, peer facilitators, and focal teachers to gather feedback and ensure that community voices are incorporated into project planning and implementation.

Learning and Knowledge Sharing

  • Document key learnings, best practices, case stories, and success stories from project activities to inform program improvements.
  • Organize learning sessions and knowledge-sharing events to disseminate lessons learned across project teams and stakeholders.
  • Contribute to the preparation of event reports and narrative reports with detailed analysis and insights.
  • Maintain an updated repository of project-related data and reports for easy access and use by the project team and partners.

Data Management and Compliance

  • Ensure accurate and timely data collection, entry, validation, and analysis in compliance with UNFPA data requirements.
  • Manage project databases and ensure data security, accuracy, and consistency.
  • Prepare quarterly progress reports, ensuring the quality and accuracy of data and narratives in line with UNFPA reporting guidelines.
  • Collaborate with the Program Manager and field staff to ensure timely submission of reports and compliance with donor requirements.
  • Ensure compliance with the Prevention of Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (PSEA) standards by integrating PSEA principles into MEAL processes and reporting mechanisms.

Capacity Building and Support

  • Provide training and technical support to project staff, peer facilitators, and community facilitators on MEAL-related tools, processes, and reporting.
  • Support focal teachers managing AFICs in tracking and reporting on student engagement and usage.
  • Facilitate orientations and capacity-building sessions for School Management Committees (SMCs) and AFIC focal teachers to improve the effective implementation of adolescent-friendly services.

Key Deliverables:

  • Evaluation reports for CSE and SRHR training sessions.
  • Quarterly Workplan Progress Reports (QWPR) as per UNFPA guidelines.
  • Documentation of case stories, success stories, and best practices.
  • Event and narrative reports for key project activities.
  • Functional and operational feedback mechanisms.
  • Updated project databases and data management systems.
  • Kobo data entry and data analysis.

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Public Health, Social Sciences, Development Studies or a related field. A Master’s degree is preferred.
  • At least 3-5 years of experience in monitoring, evaluation and learning, preferably in the fields of SRHR, gender and adolescent empowerment.
  • Experience working with UN agencies, particularly UNFPA is highly desirable.
  • Strong knowledge of MEAL tools, frameworks and methodologies.
  • Proven experience in data management, analysis and reporting.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and data analysis and Kobo tools.
  • Excellent report writing and documentation skills.
  • Strong communication, facilitation, capacity-building skills and team building skills.

Key Competencies:

  • Analytical and critical thinking.
  • Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
  • Excellent interpersonal and coordination skills.
  • Commitment to gender equality, disability inclusion and social inclusion principles.

Working Conditions:

The position will be based in Janakpur, Madhesh Province with frequent field visits to project sites.

The MEAL Coordinator will work closely with the Program Manager, project staff, community facilitators, and UNFPA representatives.

Languages: Strong command of Nepali, English, Maithili, Bhojpuri (oral and written) and local language preferred/required.

Aasaman Nepal has zero tolerance of abuse and exploitation of vulnerable people. We expect all our employees/volunteers to ensure we are protecting children, young people and vulnerable adults from harm and abide by our safeguarding policy.

Other Attributes for all positions: Excellent quality of work and commitment to high standards, strong communication skills, team building and willingness and motivated for good service delivery with strong networking and interpersonal skills and comfortable to work under stress and emergencies.

Age Limit: Candidates between 21 years to 55 years are only eligible to apply for the above position, depending upon the nature of job they will carry.

All employees must adhere to child safeguarding in person’s provisions covered in the project agreement.

Monitor staff and partners’ and ensure adherence to Aasaman Nepal’s policies and internal procedures, protocols, Aasaman Nepal’s code of Conduct with special attention to Safeguarding Standards and Procedure, Case Handling Framework, Child Protection and Do No Harm Policies. Also, immediate reporting and redress of any complaints and issues based on its severity.

Notes:

Locals, especially women and members of ethnic or marginalized communities from Madhesh Province are encouraged to apply for this position.
Applications received after the deadline will not be considered and only shortlisted candidates will be notified for the further process.
Canvassing at any stage of the recruitment process shall lead to automatic disqualification of the candidate.
Interested candidates are requested to submit their application filled by downloading the link below along with an updated CV and Cover Letter by 22/04/2026.

https://forms.gle/5rd28P1stDXBxX4s9

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for participation in the recruitment process. No telephone enquiry is entertained. ASN reserves the right to qualify/disqualify any applications without any reason.

It is intended as a guide and should not be viewed as an inflexible specification as it may vary from time to time considering the organization work. The postholder will be expected to demonstrate high level of efficient and effective working skills to perform the responsibilities.

Position Summary
To ensure compliance with ISO 14001 Environmental Management System (EMS) standards and applicable environmental regulations by monitoring, analyzing, and controlling environmental parameters within the cement plant.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Implement and maintain ISO 14001 EMS across the plant.
  • Conduct environmental monitoring of:
    • Air emissions (SPM, PM10, PM2.5, NOx, SOx)
    • Water quality (effluent & drinking water)
    • Noise levels
  • Ensure compliance with national environmental regulations and internal standards.
  • Maintain proper documentation and records for ISO audits.
  • Coordinate and support internal and external audits (ISO 14001).
  • Prepare and submit environmental reports to regulatory authorities.
  • Monitor waste management practices (hazardous & non-hazardous waste).
  • Conduct root cause analysis and corrective actions for environmental non-conformities.
  • Support sustainability initiatives like pollution reduction and resource conservation.
  • Train employees on environmental awareness and ISO requirements.

Additional Responsibilities (Preferred):

  • Assist in compliance with:
    • ISO 45001 (Occupational Health & Safety)
    • ISO 9001 (Quality Management System)
  • Monitor stack emissions and coordinate with third-party labs.
  • Ensure proper operation of pollution control equipment (ESP, Bag Filters, etc.)

Qualification:

  • Bachelor’s / Master’s Degree in Chemistry / Environmental Science / Environmental Engineering

Experience:

  • 2–5 years of experience in cement industry or heavy manufacturing
  • Hands-on experience with ISO 14001 implementation and audits

Skills Required:

  • Strong knowledge of environmental laws and standards
  • Familiarity with pollution control systems
  • Good analytical and reporting skills
  • Audit handling capability
  • Proficiency in MS Office (Excel, Word, reporting)

  • Support the collection of inputs from technical, sales, finance, legal, and project teams (e.g., resumes, case studies, certifications like ISO, technical specifications);
  • Help in formatting, editing, proofreading, and compiling bid/proposal/project sections under senior guidance;
  • Maintain document version control, organize files, and update backups to ensure accuracy;
  • Contribute to consolidating inputs from various contributors into cohesive proposal documents;
  • Assist in managing and updating the bid library/repository with templates, reusable content, past proposals, and supporting materials specific to IT tenders;
  • Work with the team in the collation of information and to ensure deadlines are met;
  • Support for the timely submission of bids physically or by uploading documents on e-tendering portal;
  • Participate in document reviews, providing inputs on clarity, grammar, and presentation;
  • Track bid-related communications, clarifications, and amendments;
  • Prepare basic supporting materials, such as executive summaries or presentations, for bid clarifications;
  • Undertake statistical analysis and research on assigned tasks;
  • Identify trends and recommend proactive or remedial action to manage business projects and plans;
  • Manage a demanding and reactive workload in a responsive way to assist the seniors to meet the team’s key objectives;
  • Gather critical information by consulting different sources to produce best report and documents for the projects;
  • Acquire knowledge of all the standards, procedures and methods that is needed for departmental duties and performance;
  • Perform other duties assigned by the seniors.

Essential skills, abilities & knowledge

  •  Should be proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint);
  •  Should possess excellent interpersonal and communication skills;
  •  Should possess the ability to cope with pressure and challenges;
  •  Should be a team player;
  • Should have excellent report writing and presentation skills.
  •  Should understand IT sector concepts (e.g., software development, cloud computing) or government procurement processes.

Academic & trades qualifications

  • BIM/BBA (Master’s preferred)

Work experience

  • Freshers

We appreciate all applications, but only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview.

Position: Software Developer Intern

Location: Kathmandu, Nepal (On-site)

No. of Requirements: 1

Position Overview:

As a Software Developer Intern, you will have the opportunity to work closely with an experienced development team on cutting-edge projects. This internship will provide you with hands-on experience in software development and exposure to industry-leading technologies.

Responsibilities:

  • Actively learn and apply software development concepts and technologies under the guidance of senior developers.
  • Participate in code reviews to improve coding skills and gain experience with relevant programming languages, frameworks, and tools.
  • Assist senior developers with assigned tasks, including bug fixes, feature development, testing, and software maintenance.
  • Work on small, independent projects to build and validate your skills.
  • Research new technologies and tools relevant to current projects.
  • Explore best practices and innovative approaches in software development.
  • Assist in creating and updating technical documentation.
  • Maintain code in version control systems.
  • Participate actively in team meetings.
  • Communicate effectively with team members and seek guidance to enhance learning and development.

Requirements:

  • Basic understanding of software development (SDLC) principles and practices.
  • Proficiency in Python, JavaScript and its associated libraries.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Knowledge of database systems, such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, is a plus.
  • Experience with version control systems, such as Git, is a plus.

What We Offer:

  • Valuable experience in a professional software development environment.
  • Learn from experienced developers and mentors.
  • Contribute to real-world projects and participate to the team’s success.
  • Develop your skills and knowledge in the latest software development technologies

We appreciate all applications, but only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview.

Job Opportunity: Account Officer

Key Responsibilities

  • Maintain and update financial records, including general ledger entries and reconciliations.

Qualifications & Requirements

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (or currently pursuing/running).

Experience: A minimum of 1 year of proven experience in an accounting or finance role is highly preferred. Fresh graduates are also encouraged to apply.

Salary: negotiable

KBA Global Chitwan is looking for a dynamic, results-driven Business Development Manager to lead student recruitment efforts and enhance brand visibility. If you are passionate about marketing, education, and strategic growth, we’d love to hear from you.

 Key Responsibilities

PHASE Nepal is a non-governmental, organization established in 2006 with a vision to build a self-empowered and self-sustained society where all kinds of discrimination are absent (please visit www.phasenepal.org for more information). PHASE Nepal’s health, education, livelihoods and research projects are currently running across various rural communities in Nepal. With support from Himalayan Development Foundation Australia (HDFA), PHASE Nepal is implementing education project in two government schools in Phaktanglung Rural Municipality in Taplejung district with the aim of improving quality of education in close collaboration with the Municipality.

We are seeking highly motivated candidates with a good academic background and experienced teaching/mentoring aiming to improve the quality of education.

Position: Teacher

Number of positions: 3 Teachers (One for Mathematics, One for English and One for Science)

Duration: 1 year with possibility of extension

Duty Station: Saraswati Chyabruk Secondary School and Sinwa Secondary School, Phaktanglung Rural Municipality, Taplejung district, Nepal

Report to: Project Manager/Program Manager

Gross Salary: NPR 1,00,000 (One Lakh only) per month (inclusive of SSF and Dashain allowance).

DELIVERABLE GOALS: MAIN TASKS, DUTIES, AND RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Provide in-class mentoring and support to respective subject teachers teaching English, Science, and Mathematics (Grades 4–10).
  • Groom, mentor, and train the teachers of Saraswati Chyabruk Secondary School and Sinwa Secondary School to strengthen teaching practices and classroom delivery following student centered activity-based teaching methods.
  • Take classes on where appropriate, especially when subject teachers are absent to demonstrate effective teaching methodologies and support student learning.
  • Support integration skills such as Creativity, Collaboration, Critical Thinking, and Communication — in classroom teaching.
  • Assist in preparing lesson plans, session plans, developing innovative teaching materials, and creating engaging learning environments.
  • Facilitate academic and co-curricular activities to promote holistic student development.
  • Maintain student assessment records and report progress to school stakeholders.
  • Support implementation of the School Improvement Plan under the supervision of the Principal and SMC.
  • Coordinate and collaborate with school staff, parents, and local stakeholders for continuous improvement.
  • Facilitate workshops, training sessions, and discussions to enhance teaching quality.
  • Promote digital learning and child-friendly, inclusive classroom practices.
  • Perform other duties as assigned by the Project Manager or school leadership.

JOB REQUIREMENTS/ REQUIRED PERSONAL ATTRIBUTES:

  • Master’s degree specializing in Mathematics.
  • Minimum of 5 years of teaching experience in secondary education, with a strong understanding of subject-specific pedagogy and classroom practices. Experience in rural or under-resourced settings will be an advantage.
  • Deep understanding of child psychology, classroom management, and effective curriculum delivery.
  • Proficiency in digital tools and communication technologies to enhance teaching and learning.
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills.
  • Patient, empathetic, adaptable, and willing to work in rural areas with minimal facilities.
  • Demonstrated passion for teaching, mentoring, and continuous professional development.

Other benefits

PHASE Nepal will provide accidental insurance of NRs. 20,00,000/- for employee and medical insurance of NRs. 2,25,000/- that covers the employee and his/her up to 2 dependent family members.

The organization will cover travel expenses for two times during the contract period.

How to Apply:

Interested candidates who meet the above-mentioned criteria are requested to submit their application by filling the form through the link https://ee-eu.kobotoolbox.org/x/Doljxx6Y no later than 30 April 2026. PHASE reserves the right to qualify/disqualify applications in any case.

We are urgently looking for these positions; therefore, we will close the application if we find appropriate candidates before the application deadline. Therefore, apply as soon as possible if you are interested.

PHASE Nepal is an equal opportunity employer and strictly follows merit-based selection. Suitably qualified local candidates, women, people with disability, and members from disadvantaged communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for the further selection process.

No telephone inquiries will be entertained, and any inappropriate recommendation will automatically disqualify the candidate from further selection process.

Human Practice Foundation (HPF) is a Danish NGO that has been working in Nepal since 2017 and Kenya since 2018. HPF aims to improve the quality of education in the public schools of rural Nepal and other parts of the world.

HPF adopts a unique approach to develop children’s potentials and create a better future for them. With our ‘child-empowerment model’ that puts the child at the centre of our intervention, HPF focuses to develop not only the academic skills but also social, emotional, cultural and interpersonal skills so that every child develops its potentials to pursue his/her career to achieve immediate and lasting change in his/her life.

HPP is looking for following different positions to work in its Project Offices:

Position: Learning Officers

Required Numbers: 8 (Eight)

Location: One of the Rural/Municipalities of Panchthar and Terhathum districts.

Qualification: Bachelor’s degree in education or equivalent with English as a major subject along with 3 years’ experience in education field related to SMC, PTA strengthening, teaching-learning pedagogy and promoting overall learning of the school children. Those holding Master degree with English as a major will be given high priority.

Job Scope: The job holder is responsible to work in a team under the leadership of Learning Manager, and to manage an educational project focused to improve students’ enrollment, retention & learning achievement in the assigned Rural/municipalities of Panchthar and Terhathum districts. Capacity building efforts aimed at strengthening School Management Committee, teachers, parents, and students are key approaches adopted by the project.

Contract Period: One year with possibility of extension on satisfactory job performance.

Persons meeting the above criteria are requested to send their application along with a short cover letter mentioning ‘suitability of candidacy’ with expected salary along with the updated CV by April 29, 2026 to vacancy.hpfnepal@gmail.com. Women candidates are encouraged to apply. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for the assessment process.

HPF adopts a fair, transparent and competency-based selection process aiming to maximise our support to the target schools. Therefore, any telephone inquiries or any undue pressure favouring the candidate shall automatically disqualify the candidacy.

HPF Nepal reserves all right to select or not toselect candidates through this vacancy.

International Nepal Fellowship (Nepal) is a Non-Government Organization established in 1952 and serves the poor and disadvantaged people of Nepal to enable life to its fullness through health and development services. INF Nepal is currently working in health and community development services in the Gandaki, Lumbini, Karnali, and Sudurpashchim Provinces of Nepal.

For the following position, INF Nepal Green Pastures Hospital is seeking competent, skilled, service oriented, and devoted Nepali citizen who shares our vision, mission, and values.

The job holder is responsible for effective execution of burn care trainings, identification and arrange treatment for patients with post burn complications and conduct awareness campaigns. The job holder serves as an interface between community and clinical team, ensuring alignment with project objectives.

Required Qualifications, Skills, and Knowledge:

Position: Field Assistant (After Sales)

Locations: Achham (Candidates may be deployed to any municipality within the Achham district based on project requirements).

No. of Vacancy/s: [1]

Duration: Up to September 2026

Report to: Cluster Field Officer

Purpose of Position:

Field Assistant will be responsible for cook stove monitoring, household visit and interaction on usage of stoves, provide repair and maintenance services, data collection in assigned location. S/he will work closely with cluster field officer coordinating with local government, ward office, line agencies and local NGOs/CBOs. S/he will perform the following specific tasks,

Required qualification and experience:

  • 10+2 Passed with at least 2 years experiences in social mobilization.
  • Good interpersonal skills and the ability to work effectively with community people in coordination with all sub sector staff at cluster and local level.
  • Experience on community level mobilization, implementation and facilitation to conduct various sessions would be added advantage.
  • Working knowledge in computer and mobile phone-based surveying will be an added advantage.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support the implementation of the project’s operational plan under the TTCM project, specifically focusing on post-sales interventions.
  • Conduct household visits to interact with cook stove users, provide guidance on usage, and collect relevant feedback and data.
  • Deliver repair and maintenance services to cook stove users in multiple communities throughout the district to ensure continued functionality and user satisfaction
  • Coordinate effectively with beneficiaries, FCHVs (Female Community Health Volunteers), and other local stakeholders.
  • Verify cookstove sales and usage through door-to-door visits and phone interviews with a randomly selected 30% sample of households, using KoBoToolbox for monitoring.
  • Assist in organizing and facilitating workshops, seminars, and trainings related to after-sales services at the field level.
  • Support capacity building of user households to ensure the sustained and efficient use of cookstoves.
  • Encourage and facilitate local entrepreneurs and distributors to promote community-level engagement and sustainability.
  • Conduct regular visits to program areas, ensuring strong coordination and linkages with local stakeholders.
  • Facilitate the project and stakeholder team during field visit.
  • Collect of data and information and prepare monthly, quarterly progress and event-based report.
  • Prepare the case study, success story and feature etc.
  • Share the learning and best practices to other related staffs and support for the better performance.
  • Coordinate and direct report to Cluster Field Officer (CFO) with project team regularly.
  • Fulfill other specific assignments given by ASTHA Nepal.

Organizational Policies, Safeguarding, and Code of Conduct:

  • Ensure that all involved acting on the organization’s behalf are aware of the Safeguarding Policy, Diversity, and Dignity in the Workplace Policy, Code of Conduct, Fraud Policy, and Whistleblowing policy.
  • Ensure beneficiaries whom we work with are aware of the safeguarding policy including the reporting lines when appropriate.
  • Be responsible that anyone acting on our behalf has signed up to the Safeguarding and Code of Conduct policies.
  • Ensure our ethics and values, as set out in our Code of Conduct and related policies, including safeguarding, are embedded in team culture, and well-modelled by others. Ensure that reporting structures are well promoted and respond to all concerns appropriately.
  • Responsible for gender-responsive behaviour in all actions and decisions. Ensure non-discriminative behaviour based on gender, age, sex, race, ethnic background, culture, disability, nationality, religion, and marital status. Is sensitive and adaptable to gender and social inclusion
